mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals strong labor market demand, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days through corporate earnings optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS breakout periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) indicates robust demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through sectoral momentum.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving HOUST/RSAFS periods are negative or pairwise correlation < 0.3 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals supply-side disruption, causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days through inflationary pressure and earnings uncertainty.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO contraction periods are positive or Rank IC > 0.05 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 203.15 (CPIAUCSL) triggers central bank tightening, leading to S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days through higher discount rates.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in CPIAUCSL breakout periods are positive or Rank IC < -0.03 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate (UNRATE) decline below 4.5% signals labor market tightening, generating S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days through wage inflation expectations.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE decline periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.02 in 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% indicates strong economic momentum, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 60 trading days through corporate investment optimism.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 breakout periods are negative or Rank IC < 0.04 in 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signal supply-side weakness, causing S&P 500 negative returns over 20 trading days through sectoral demand erosion.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in co-contraction INDPRO/HOUST periods are positive or pairwise correlation > 0.5 in 3-year windows.
